title: Clusia engleriana
intro: Clusia engleriana, also known as the Clusia tree or Clusia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Clusiaceae. The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Clusia engleriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Clusia engleriana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive problems to infections.
taxonomy: Clusia engleriana is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Clusiaceae. This family is part of the order Malpighiales within the clade rosids. The order Malpighiales is a part of the subclass Rosidae in the class Magnoliopsida, which is the flowering plant division in the kingdom Plantae. The kingdom Plantae is further classified into the domain Eukaryota. This comprehensive classification reflects the evolutionary history and relationships of the plant species.
uses: Clusia engleriana is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to their anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, Clusia engleriana's roots are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
benefits: Clusia engleriana has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Latin America, due to its potential health benefits.
